observed: operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is a canonical snapshot updated before later additive queue-update commits
M-011 hands off a deterministic materializer requirement
inferred: snapshot-only selection can omit later durable queue history
proposed: materialize state only from an explicit update set and keep authoritative=false until complete enumeration is attested
superseded: treating ACTIVE_QUEUE.json alone as exhaustive current queue state
unresolved: complete repository enumeration of operations/queue-updates was not proven in this run
cryptographic binding of the explicit input manifest is not yet implemented

verification.runtime: Node.js test runner against byte-equivalent committed source
covered: non-authoritative default and explicit authority flag

privacy_boundary: Only public repository metadata, queue structure, commit identifiers, and operational claim states are retained. No raw chat, health, credentials, contacts, account identifiers, or unreviewed uploads are included.
trust_limits: A caller-provided enumerationComplete flag is an attestation, not independent proof of exhaustive repository enumeration.
Passing deterministic tests does not prove the supplied update set is complete or authentic.
falsification_route: Enumerate the repository tree independently, compare every operations/queue-updates path with the explicit manifest, and demonstrate either an omitted event or an event-order collision.
